[2020-04-26 23:31] velizar_ (Name): yeah, I don't think it's any good to have a fragile blocker which doesn't have a click ability or regen.
[2020-04-26 23:32] velizar_ (Name): or well, it can lose its blocker status after it blocks once.
[2020-04-26 23:33] velizar_ (Name): but then you'll want to click it every turn
[2020-04-26 23:33] velizar_ (Name): seems pointless, the only benefit of this design over a design which requires you to click to gain the :Attack: is on the first turn.
[2020-04-26 23:34] velizar_ (Name): then you'd be able to both attack and defend
[2020-04-26 23:34] velizar_ (Name): for every other turn, you only attack without defending
[2020-04-26 23:34] velizar_ (Name): it's just a colossus

[2020-04-26 23:35] velizar_ (Name): this is also worse against freeze & flexible attack. But that's too situational.
[2020-04-26 23:37] zera777 (Zera): Colossus normally doesn't attack on it's first turn out.
This unit does.
For 1 damage. 🤔
[2020-04-26 23:39] velizar_ (Name): the problem is that if you have a :Blocker: unit with 1 hp which you never want to block with, that will only confuse you when calculating defense
[2020-04-26 23:39] velizar_ (Name): because you never want to defend with that unit
[2020-04-26 23:40] velizar_ (Name): or almost never. in 99.9999% of the time you don't want to defend with that unit.

[2020-04-26 23:41] velizar_ (Name): only [[Sentry]] has that issue. And forcefields when they attack.
[2020-04-26 23:42] velizar_ (Name): and soaking on borehole isn't TOO bad, it's as bad as soaking on steelsplitter.
[2020-04-26 23:44] zera777 (Zera): Isn't soaking Borehole even worse than soaking Steelsplitter?
Paying an attacker for 2 health must be worse than paying an attacker for 3 health.
[2020-04-26 23:46] velizar_ (Name): in order to soak splitter, you have to hold it - holding it costs you 1 attack
[2020-04-26 23:47] zera777 (Zera): Ah, that makes sense.
[2020-04-26 23:47] velizar_ (Name): well... ok, 1 hp and 1 attack are not equal
[2020-04-26 23:47] velizar_ (Name): but they are approximately equal. it depends on the situation

[2020-04-27 09:15] velizar_ (Name): Breaking SDR just a little is fine.
[2020-04-27 09:15] velizar_ (Name): It's not an absolute rule, especially for already existing rules.
[2020-04-27 09:17] velizar_ (Name): You can do something with absorb without breaking SDR:
[2020-04-27 09:19] velizar_ (Name): A wall with 4 hp which gives you 1 gold per unabsorbed hp. So if you don't get any damage you get 3 gold, if you get hit for 2 and absorb on the wall you get 1 gold
[2020-04-27 09:19] velizar_ (Name): But that's really situational: you will always want to absorb
[2020-04-27 09:20] velizar_ (Name): Or... If you absorb on a different unit, then you get the full gold.
[2020-04-27 09:20] velizar_ (Name): That can be interesting. It becomes equivalent to a vigilant gold producer.
[2020-04-27 09:23] shadourow (Rando): Nice, a discount drone !
[2020-04-27 09:24] shadourow (Rando): Or a really overpriced wall

[2020-05-02 16:12] flakmaniak: If there were a unit for 2B, non-prompt 2HP blocker, would that get bought very much? I assume not, since 2B is ~2.66 at Drone inflation, so close to the price of two Engineers. And it's less granular.

messages.author: flakmaniak
messages.content: If there were a unit for 2B, non-prompt 2HP blocker, would that get bought very much? I assume not, since 2B is ~2.66 at Drone inflation, so close to the price of two Engineers. And it's less granular.

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (18 messages, 2020-05-02) ---
[2020-05-02 16:38] anesotericman (Esoteric): more efficient than engineers or forcefields, and is a tech sink, but never your goal. Would see similar buy rates as oxide mixer, probably.
[2020-05-02 16:39] flakmaniak: Right. It's never a unit you pin your strategy on, but you might buy them out of convenience sometimes.
[2020-05-02 16:39] flakmaniak: I wonder how much design space there is for very efficient non-prompt soak units.
[2020-05-02 16:39] flakmaniak: If you made a 1B non-prompt blocker with 2HP, what would that end up being like?
[2020-05-02 16:40] flakmaniak: I guess being so cheap for the tech makes it play really differently than e.g. a 6B non-prompt thing that makes 5HP of soak.
[2020-05-02 16:40] anesotericman (Esoteric): chieftain, hannibull, and thunderhead play in this space
[2020-05-02 16:40] flakmaniak: (So as not to make it Fragile, nor a giant absorber, I guess it makes a 3HP and a 2HP blocker.)
[2020-05-02 16:43] anesotericman (Esoteric): those three all make it more interesting than just attackers being bad though, as they reward finesse to avoid absorb
[2020-05-02 16:43] anesotericman (Esoteric): as opposed to just making the best strat being buying all the drones every time
[2020-05-02 16:44] anesotericman (Esoteric): not that the 6b for 5 would do that
[2020-05-02 16:44] flakmaniak: I just like the idea of non-prompt soakers that are really strong, so you try to stay a turn ahead on defense, pre-buying them. And then eventually you have to fall back on prompt defense when things get harder.
[2020-05-02 16:45] flakmaniak: So, a Xeno/Infusion-type thing, but tuned to be a little more soak per-dollar, but not a strong absorber.
[2020-05-02 16:45] anesotericman (Esoteric): chieftains are what you're looking for
[2020-05-02 16:46] anesotericman (Esoteric): except they can be 1-3 turns ahead and can be exploited
[2020-05-02 16:46] anesotericman (Esoteric): which makes it a big more interesting, imo
[2020-05-02 16:47] anesotericman (Esoteric): although the situation you're describing often happens infusion grids in sets with only walls and forcefield for prompt defense, for supply reasons
[2020-05-02 16:48] anesotericman (Esoteric): more room to play in the area, to be sure, but that's where that gameplay currently resides
[2020-05-02 16:48] anesotericman (Esoteric): ossified drones, perforators, corpus clicks

[2020-05-07 20:16] velizar_ (Name): I tend to include context.
[2020-05-07 20:17] velizar_ (Name): One thing is absorb for the purposes of absorb denial - in that case, barrier is "absorb"
[2020-05-07 20:18] velizar_ (Name): Another thing is absorb for the purposes of getting a good absorber in the set, since having an absorber is really cost-efficient - in that case, barrier is not "absorb", you still need a wall. In that sense, a wall is about as good as a permanent attacker with 2 :Attack:.
[2020-05-07 20:27] archagent (JackNoir): When it comes to things like Innervi, absorb has to be calculated retroactively. You can't buy it and say "I now have 2 absorb".

Say you buy it, then use it to block 2 damage. Two turns later, you use the whole thing to soak. In this case, I'd say you got two absorb out of it. Those two damage your opponent dealt the first turn had 0 effect.

Plex/Barrier isn't absorb, because you only buy it when you need it. If your opponent forces you to spend resources on a barrier to prevent a breach, they still got value out of their attack
